purpose: Queue the official map, flow, aerial, public-media, social-link, and first-party capture work needed to move South Fork American, Colorado River, and Pacuare from source-aware preview captures toward lifelike Unreal environments without importing unreviewed third-party photos, video frames, captions, audio, comments, or model-training data.

web_reviewed_evidence: official .gov page for the South Fork American recreation corridor
page lists access points including Chili Bar, Henningsen-Lotus Park, Highway 49 Bridge West, Marshall Gold Discovery State Historical Park, Camp Lotus, Greenwood Creek, and Salmon Falls Bridge
page links a full-screen map and general map PDF lead
page includes BLM-captioned South Fork American River photo leads
page footer exposes BLM social channels including YouTube, Instagram, and Flickr for manual link-only search followup
reviewed_official_source_leads.next_action: Attach exact map/PDF metadata, current access restrictions, photo item credits, social item URLs, and guide/outfitter validation before deriving route geometry or visual reference targets.
reviewed_official_source_leads.promotion_gate: Do not digitize access geometry or use any BLM-hosted/social image as art input until current terms, attribution, publication sensitivity, and item-level rights are recorded.

web_reviewed_evidence: station AMERICAN RIVER AT CHILI BAR (CBR) is in the American River basin
page exposes hourly and 15-minute stage/flow/reservoir-elevation tables
page links CDEC JSON/CSV historical data services and station metadata lookup
page labels displayed data provisional and subject to change
reviewed_official_source_leads.next_action: Pull reviewed CSV/JSON windows for low, summer-commercial, and high/runnable conditions, then bind water width, wet-bank, foam, holes, laterals, and rescue readability variants to explicit date/time flow bands.
reviewed_official_source_leads.promotion_gate: Do not promote a South Fork flow preset until CDEC data window, units, provisional/final status, powerhouse/release context, and guide interpretation are recorded.

web_reviewed_evidence: official .gov river trips and permits page for Grand Canyon National Park
page describes Lees Ferry to Diamond Creek commercial and noncommercial whitewater trips
page includes official rafting and Colorado River below Nankoweap photo leads with NPS credit lines
page footer exposes NPS social channels including YouTube, Instagram, and Flickr for manual link-only search followup
reviewed_official_source_leads.next_action: Attach river-mile, launch/takeout, photo item metadata, public-screenshot sensitivity, and oarsman/guide review before using this source in route or art decisions.
reviewed_official_source_leads.promotion_gate: Use only as official context until item-level media rights, route geometry, and river-mile relation are accepted.

web_reviewed_evidence: page advertises a digital photo archive with more than 5000 Grand Canyon photos in the public domain or Creative Commons Attribution
page advertises public-domain b-roll video footage for multimedia use
page links webcams and virtual tours useful for atmosphere and lighting review
page footer exposes official NPS social/photo channels for manual link-only discovery
reviewed_official_source_leads.next_action: Select exact river-corridor archive items, then record creator, date, license, credit line, location/river mile, flow context if available, and permitted derivative use before art reference or texture-study promotion.
reviewed_official_source_leads.promotion_gate: No frame, photo, or clip may become a material, photogrammetry, training, or packaged reference input until item-level metadata and attribution are attached.

web_reviewed_evidence: official Glen Canyon Dam water operations page
page records current operations, monthly release context, and operational-tier context
page notes daily scheduled fluctuations and instantaneous release adjustments can affect downstream flow
page links the USGS Colorado River at Lees Ferry downstream streamflow station
reviewed_official_source_leads.next_action: Pair USBR monthly/daily release context with USGS Lees Ferry/Grand Canyon gauge histories and Grand Canyon rapid review before tuning rowing-current, eddyline, wave-train, and flip/pin flow variants.
reviewed_official_source_leads.promotion_gate: Do not promote Colorado flow visuals or feature forcing until release schedule, gauge lag, river-mile reach, and oarsman review are recorded.

reviewed_official_source_leads.provider: Sistema Nacional de Informacion Territorial de Costa Rica / IDECORI
web_reviewed_evidence: official Costa Rica OGC service catalog page
page explains WMS, WMTS, and WFS service types
page notes CRTM05, CR05/CRTM05, and WGS84 projection availability
page lists orthophoto, model, MINAE Direccion de Agua, IMN, SINAC, SENARA, ICE, and ICT node leads
reviewed_official_source_leads.next_action: Select exact Pacuare corridor layers for orthophoto, hydrology, forest cover, protected area, rainfall/stations, and tourism context; capture layer metadata, CRS, terms, responsible institution, and corridor intersection before import.
reviewed_official_source_leads.promotion_gate: Do not import or package SNIT layers until layer-level terms, CRS, attribution, metadata date, corridor relevance, and Costa Rica guide/geospatial review pass.

search_targets: Chili Bar rapid South Fork American River
Troublemaker rapid South Fork American River
Satan's Cesspool South Fork American River
South Fork American River rafting high water low water
Lees Ferry rowing raft Grand Canyon
Colorado River Grand Canyon Nankoweap rapid
Colorado River Grand Canyon muddy release
Grand Canyon rowing eddyline wave train
Grand Canyon Colorado River sandbar camp low water
Pacuare River rainforest waterfalls low water high water
Rio Pacuare rafting Siquirres Finca La Cruz

american_south_fork: Attach BLM map/PDF metadata and reconcile with existing NHD/NAIP/3DEP stationing.
Pull CDEC CBR historical CSV/JSON windows for low, commercial, and high flow examples.
Collect link-only BLM/Wikimedia/Flickr/social rapid references and request explicit rights for any candidate production reference item.
Generate first-party procedural canyon-bank, wet-rock, grass/scrub, and foam/wetness variants from the reviewed flow bands.
colorado_river: Pair NPS route/river-mile context with USBR Glen Canyon release context and USGS Lees Ferry/Grand Canyon gauges.
Select public-domain or CC-BY NPS archive items with exact credits and river-mile/rapid evidence.
Collect link-only private-boater/social rowing references and request explicit rights for any candidate production reference item.
Generate first-party procedural canyon strata, sandbar, muddy-water, eddyline, wave-train, and oar-rig variants from accepted release bands.
pacuare: Select Pacuare-relevant SNIT orthophoto, model, Direccion de Agua, IMN, SINAC, SENARA, ICE, and ICT layers with CRS and terms.
Collect link-only rainforest gorge, waterfall, Huacas, Double Drop, and Cimarones visual references with guide/outfitter review.
Request explicit rights for any social/photo/video item before use beyond manual review questions.
Generate first-party procedural rainforest canopy, moss/wet-rock, waterfall mist, muddy-clear flow, and leaf-litter variants from accepted source masks and rainfall/flow bands.

next_checkpoint: Promote exact source layers and media items into river manifests only after metadata, terms, attribution, location, flow context, guide review, and capture/regression evidence are attached.
